research_question: After the Alpha158 confirmatory fine-tuning line was paused because CORD30 failed the final turnover-control-only follow-up, can one small dual-signal family built from the reserve head-extraction pair corr30 and cord30 smooth cross-sectional rank movement enough to reduce the structural turnover problem of single-signal TopK equal-weight selection, while preserving higher invested-weight head-extraction characteristics?
lineage_note: This is a new exploratory family-construction line. It does not reopen the paused Alpha158 confirmatory fine-tuning line.

time_partition_plan.note: The frozen 2022-2025 test window remains excluded by construction through the trainval-only snapshot.

feature_set.interpretation: stronger 30-day price-level and log-volume co-movement should rank higher
stronger 30-day price-change and volume-change co-movement should rank higher

score_family_contract.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(alpha158_corr30_raw DESC), percentile_rank(alpha158_cord30_raw DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 2
score_family_contract.missing_data_rule: Only score rows with at least 2 non-null ranked features; otherwise emit null model_score_D0 and preserve the row for audit.

combination_rationale.why_not_vsumd60_first: VSUMD60 remains a reserve atomic keeper, but its bottom-avoidance / cash-drag profile is intentionally excluded from the first multi-signal head-extraction family seed.
combination_rationale.mechanism_hypothesis: corr30 and cord30 are mechanism-near but not identical reserve cards. Blending level-correlation and change-correlation should smooth boundary rank movement more than either single signal alone, reducing turnover without immediately reverting to a defensive bottom-avoidance profile.

constants_frozen.selection_contract: freeze the simpler standalone-family operating contract used by the CORD30 confirmatory line: no added liquidity guard, TopK=10, and cash allowed when names are not selected

success_diagnostics: Compare turnover compression versus standalone CORD30 under the same full-refresh equal-weight contract.
Compare whether avg_invested_weight stays at or above the higher-deployment regime established by CORD30.
Compare whether the family remains better than v18 on annual_relative_return, relative_ir, and max_drawdown.
Inspect whether the family reduces boundary instability enough that later refresh control might become unnecessary or materially weaker.
disallowed_changes: Do not reopen Alpha158 confirmatory strict recheck or family-seed status inside this round.
Do not add VSUMD60, IMAX20, IMXD5, or any third signal into this first dual-signal family seed.
Do not add turnover-control hysteresis, extraction smoothing, or weight tilts inside this round.
Do not change TopK width, benchmark, execution semantics, or cost assumptions.
Do not sweep multiple aggregation formulas or multiple dual-signal pairs inside this round.
notes: This round is opened only after the Alpha158 confirmatory fine-tuning line was formally paused.
The goal is not to continue local micro-tuning, but to test a new structural hypothesis: multi-signal blending itself may reduce single-signal structural turnover.
The first exploratory family is intentionally minimal: only corr30 and cord30, no third reserve card, no overlay, and no refresh-rule intervention.
